recipes.title: Pad Thai
recipes.description: Stir-fried rice noodles with shrimp, tofu, egg, bean sprouts and tamarind sauce, topped with peanuts.
steps: Soak noodles in warm water for 20 minutes until pliable, then drain.
Whisk tamarind, fish sauce and palm sugar to make pad thai sauce.
Heat oil in a wok and fry diced tofu until golden, then add chopped garlic and shrimp.
Push to the side and crack eggs in, scramble briefly.
Add noodles and sauce, tossing for 2 minutes until absorbed.
Add bean sprouts and chives, toss 30 seconds and serve with crushed peanuts and lime.

recipes.title: Tom Yum Goong
recipes.description: Hot and sour Thai shrimp soup fragrant with lemongrass, kaffir lime, galangal and chilli.
steps: Peel shrimp keeping the heads and shells, simmer shells with water for 15 minutes for stock, then strain.
Smash lemongrass, galangal and lime leaves and add to the stock.
Bring to a boil and add halved mushrooms and tomato wedges.
Add shrimp and cook 2 minutes until just opaque.
Off heat, stir in fish sauce, nam prik pao, lime juice and crushed chillies.
Garnish with cilantro and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Som Tum
recipes.description: Spicy green papaya salad pounded with chilli, lime, fish sauce, peanuts and tomatoes.
steps: Peel papaya and shred into thin strips.
Pound garlic and chillies in a mortar until coarse.
Add palm sugar, fish sauce, lime juice and tamarind, pounding to dissolve.
Add dried shrimp and peanuts and pound briefly.
Add long beans cut into 3 cm pieces and tomatoes, bruise gently.
Add papaya and use a spoon to fold and pound lightly until dressed.

recipes.title: Massaman Curry
recipes.description: Rich southern Thai curry of beef, potatoes and peanuts in coconut milk with warm spices.
steps: Cube beef and brown in a heavy pot.
Crack open coconut cream from the top of the can and fry curry paste in it for 5 minutes.
Add beef, remaining coconut milk, cinnamon, star anise and cardamom.
Simmer covered for 90 minutes until beef is tender.
Add potatoes, onions and peanuts and simmer 25 minutes more.
Season with tamarind, palm sugar and fish sauce to balance.

recipes.title: Khao Pad
recipes.description: Thai-style fried rice with shrimp, egg, garlic, scallions and a squeeze of lime.
steps: Use day-old chilled rice, breaking up clumps by hand.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat and stir-fry chopped garlic and onion 30 seconds.
Add shrimp and cook 1 minute, push aside and scramble eggs into curds.
Add rice and toss for 2 minutes.
Season with fish sauce, soy sauce and sugar, tossing to coat.
Finish with scallions and serve with lime wedges and cucumber slices.

recipes.title: Pad See Ew
recipes.description: Wide rice noodles charred with chicken, Chinese broccoli and sweet dark soy sauce.
steps: Slice chicken and noodles into bite-sized pieces, separating the noodles by hand.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat and stir-fry garlic and chicken 2 minutes.
Push to one side, crack in eggs and scramble briefly.
Add Chinese broccoli stems first, then leaves, stirring 1 minute.
Add noodles and sauces, allowing them to char on the wok bottom.
Toss everything together, finish with white pepper.

recipes.title: Tom Kha Gai
recipes.description: Coconut and galangal chicken soup balanced with lime, lemongrass and chilli.
steps: Slice galangal thinly and bruise lemongrass and lime leaves.
Combine coconut milk and stock with the aromatics and bring to a gentle simmer.
Simmer 10 minutes to infuse.
Add sliced chicken and mushrooms, simmer 8 minutes until cooked.
Off heat, season with fish sauce, palm sugar, lime juice and crushed chillies.
Top with cilantro and serve.

recipes.title: Larb
recipes.description: Northeastern Thai minced meat salad with toasted rice powder, herbs, lime and chilli.
steps: Toast glutinous rice in a dry pan until deep golden, then grind to a coarse powder.
In a wok, cook chicken with a splash of water, breaking it up.
Off heat, season chicken with fish sauce, lime juice, sugar and chilli flakes.
Stir in sliced shallots, scallions and toasted rice powder.
Fold through mint and cilantro just before serving.
Serve with lettuce leaves and steamed sticky rice.

recipes.title: Khao Soi
recipes.description: Northern Thai coconut curry noodle soup topped with crispy egg noodles, pickles and lime.
steps: Fry curry paste in 2 tablespoons oil until fragrant.
Add coconut milk and stock, bring to a simmer.
Add chicken pieces and simmer 25 minutes until cooked through.
Season with fish sauce and palm sugar.
Cook two-thirds of the noodles in boiling water, then divide among bowls.
Deep-fry remaining noodles in hot oil for 30 seconds until crisp.
Ladle curry over noodles, top with crispy noodles, shallots, pickles, cilantro and lime.

recipes.title: Green Curry
recipes.description: Fragrant Thai green curry with chicken, Thai eggplant, basil and coconut milk.
steps: Crack open coconut cream from the top of the can and simmer in a wok until oil separates.
Add curry paste and fry until very fragrant, 3 minutes.
Add sliced chicken and toss to coat with paste.
Pour in remaining coconut milk and lime leaves, simmer 8 minutes.
Add quartered eggplant and bamboo shoots, simmer 8 more minutes.
Season with fish sauce and palm sugar, finish with Thai basil and chilli slices.

recipes.title: Pad Krapow
recipes.description: Stir-fried minced pork with holy basil, chilli and garlic, served over rice with a fried egg.
steps: Pound garlic and chillies into a coarse paste.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a wok and fry the paste 30 seconds.
Add pork and stir-fry 4 minutes, breaking up.
Add chopped long beans and stir-fry 1 minute.
Season with oyster sauce, fish sauce, soy sauce and sugar.
Toss in holy basil leaves until just wilted.
In a separate pan, fry eggs in hot oil until edges are crisp.
Serve pork over rice topped with the fried egg.

recipes.title: Mango Sticky Rice
recipes.description: Sweet coconut-soaked sticky rice served with ripe mango and toasted mung beans.
steps: Soak glutinous rice in water for at least 4 hours.
Drain rice and steam in a bamboo basket lined with cloth for 25 minutes.
Warm coconut milk with sugar, salt and pandan leaf until dissolved.
Pour two-thirds of the coconut mixture over the hot rice and let it absorb 20 minutes.
Slice the mango cheeks.
Plate rice with mango, drizzle with the remaining coconut sauce and sprinkle mung beans.

recipes.title: Panang Curry
recipes.description: Thick, peanut-rich Thai curry with beef, kaffir lime and coconut cream.
steps: Slice beef thinly against the grain.
Simmer the cream from the top of the coconut milk in a wok until oil separates.
Add curry paste and ground peanuts, fry 3 minutes.
Add beef and toss to coat.
Pour in remaining coconut milk and shredded lime leaves, simmer 8 minutes.
Season with fish sauce and palm sugar, garnish with sliced chilli and basil.

recipes.title: Yam Nua
recipes.description: Spicy Thai grilled beef salad with cucumber, tomato, mint and a tangy lime dressing.
steps: Season steak with salt and pepper and grill to medium-rare.
Rest 5 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain.
Whisk lime juice, fish sauce, palm sugar, minced garlic and crushed chillies for the dressing.
Combine sliced steak with halved tomatoes, sliced cucumber and shallots.
Pour over dressing and toss gently.
Fold through mint and cilantro just before serving.

recipes.title: Pad Woon Sen
recipes.description: Glass noodle stir-fry with shrimp, chicken, eggs and vegetables in a savoury sauce.
steps: Soak glass noodles in warm water for 10 minutes, then drain.
Heat oil in a wok and stir-fry chopped garlic with sliced chicken for 2 minutes.
Add shrimp and cook 1 minute, push aside and scramble eggs.
Add julienned carrot and shredded cabbage and stir 1 minute.
Add noodles, oyster sauce, soy sauce, fish sauce and sugar with 80 ml water.
Toss until noodles are translucent and tender, finish with scallions.

recipes.title: Khao Man Gai
recipes.description: Thai-style Hainanese chicken rice with poached chicken and fragrant chicken-fat rice.
steps: Poach the whole chicken with smashed ginger, garlic and cilantro roots for 35 minutes; rest in stock.
Reserve 1 litre stock and cool, slice cooled chicken.
Saute crushed garlic and ginger in chicken fat, add rinsed rice and toss 2 minutes.
Cook rice with chicken stock, pandan leaf and salt until fluffy.
Pound chillies, garlic and ginger, then mix with soybean paste, soy sauce and vinegar for sauce.
Serve sliced chicken over rice with cucumber, sauce and a bowl of broth.

recipes.title: Pad Kee Mao
recipes.description: Drunken stir-fried wide rice noodles with shrimp, holy basil, chillies and garlic.
steps: Pound garlic and chillies into a paste.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat and fry the paste 30 seconds.
Add shrimp and stir-fry 1 minute, then push aside.
Add long beans and bell pepper and stir 1 minute.
Add noodles, oyster sauce, soy sauces, fish sauce and sugar, tossing to char.
Finish with handfuls of holy basil and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Satay
recipes.description: Thai-style grilled marinated chicken skewers served with creamy peanut sauce.
steps: Soak skewers in water for 30 minutes.
Slice chicken into thin strips and marinate with half the coconut milk, lemongrass, spices, garlic and fish sauce.
Thread chicken onto skewers and grill over hot coals 3 minutes per side, basting with coconut milk.
For peanut sauce, fry curry paste in remaining coconut milk in a saucepan.
Add ground peanuts, palm sugar, tamarind and a splash of water and simmer to thick sauce.
Serve skewers with peanut sauce.

recipes.title: Yum Woon Sen
recipes.description: Glass noodle salad tossed with shrimp, ground pork, peanuts and a tart lime dressing.
steps: Soak glass noodles in warm water for 8 minutes, then blanch briefly and drain.
Boil pork briefly in water, add shrimp and cook until just done, drain.
Pound chillies and garlic in a mortar.
Mix with lime juice, fish sauce and palm sugar to make dressing.
Toss noodles, pork, shrimp, sliced shallots, celery, tomato wedges and dressing.
Top with peanuts and cilantro.

recipes.title: Khao Niao
recipes.description: Steamed Thai sticky rice, the staple accompaniment to northern and Isan dishes.
steps: Rinse rice and soak in water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Drain the rice and place in a bamboo steamer basket lined with cloth.
Set basket over a pot of boiling water.
Steam for 20 minutes, then flip the rice over.
Steam another 10 minutes until rice is glossy and chewy.
Transfer to a covered basket to keep warm and serve with mains.

recipes.title: Gai Yang
recipes.description: Thai grilled marinated chicken with garlic, cilantro root, fish sauce and white pepper.
steps: Butterfly the chicken by removing the backbone and pressing flat.
Pound garlic, cilantro roots, peppercorns and lemongrass to a paste.
Mix paste with fish sauce, soy sauce, palm sugar and coconut milk.
Rub marinade thoroughly into the chicken and refrigerate at least 4 hours.
Grill skin-side up over medium coals for 35 minutes, turning often and basting.
Rest 10 minutes, chop into pieces and serve with sticky rice.

recipes.title: Pad Prik King
recipes.description: Stir-fried green beans and pork with red curry paste and kaffir lime leaves.
steps: Slice pork thinly against the grain and cut beans into 4 cm pieces.
Heat oil in a wok and fry curry paste until fragrant, 2 minutes.
Add pork and stir-fry 3 minutes until cooked.
Add beans and toss 3 minutes until just tender-crisp.
Season with fish sauce, soy sauce and palm sugar.
Finish with shredded lime leaves and sliced chilli.

recipes.title: Khanom Krok
recipes.description: Thai coconut pancakes cooked in a special pan with crisp edges and creamy custard centres.
steps: Blend rice flour, cooked rice, half the coconut milk and a pinch of salt into a smooth batter; rest 1 hour.
Mix remaining coconut milk with sugar and remaining salt for the topping.
Heat a khanom krok pan and brush each well with oil.
Fill each well two-thirds with batter and cook 2 minutes until edges set.
Top with sweet topping and add corn or scallion to each.
Cover briefly until tops are creamy, then scoop out two pancakes joined together.

recipes.title: Hoy Tod
recipes.description: Thai crispy oyster omelette with a chewy batter, bean sprouts and chilli sauce.
steps: Whisk rice flour, tapioca starch and water into a smooth batter.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil on a flat griddle and pour batter to form a thin pancake.
Scatter oysters across the batter and cook until edges are crisp.
Pour beaten eggs over the top and cook 2 minutes.
Top with bean sprouts and scallions, drizzle with soy sauce.
Slide onto a plate and serve with sriracha.

recipes.title: Nam Tok
recipes.description: Thai grilled beef salad with toasted rice powder, lime, fish sauce, mint and chilli.
steps: Toast glutinous rice in a dry pan until golden, then grind to powder.
Grill steak to medium-rare and rest 5 minutes.
Slice steak thinly against the grain.
Whisk lime juice, fish sauce, palm sugar and chilli flakes into a dressing.
Toss steak with dressing, sliced shallots and toasted rice powder.
Fold in mint, cilantro and scallions and serve with sticky rice.

recipes.title: Pla Rad Prik
recipes.description: Whole crispy fish topped with a sweet, tart and spicy tamarind chilli sauce.
steps: Score the fish on both sides and dust thoroughly with tapioca starch.
Heat oil to 180C and deep-fry the fish 6-7 minutes per side until crisp.
Pound garlic, chillies and cilantro roots into a paste.
Fry the paste in 2 tablespoons oil for 1 minute.
Add tamarind, palm sugar, fish sauce and 60 ml water and simmer to a syrup.
Stir in lime juice, pour over the fried fish and garnish with cilantro.
